page: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dedicated x86_64 architecture resources. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 128 physical cores and 256 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per physical core to support intensive data processing tasks.

The server includes eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, delivering a total of 1536 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage consists of 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, which provides high-speed local data access. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included with the deployment.

This bare metal configuration represents a high-density hardware class designed for extreme computational workloads. The integration of massive system memory, extensive local NVMe storage, and eight Blackwell GPUs makes it highly resource-dense, reducing the need for multi-node clustering for large-scale models. It is built for workloads such as large language model training, complex deep learning tasks, high-performance computing, large-scale data science jobs, and massive database deployments.
description: Vultr vbm-256c-3072gb-8-b200-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for intensive computational workloads. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 128 physical cores and 256 dedicated vCPUs operating at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core, and a massive local storage capacity of 28608 GB NVMe SSD. For acceleration, the server is equipped with eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. This resource-dense profile is optimized for large-scale artificial intelligence training, deep learning, high-performance computing, and massive database operations.
